MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiffs Claim Or Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Sep-26-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SPX COOLING TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiffs Claim Or Punitive Damages

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant SPX Cooling Technologies, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment and, in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication of Plaintiffs' Claim for Punitive Damages is off calendar. Dismissal filed as to SPX Cooling Technologies, Inc. on August 6, 2019.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Not reported. (503/CML)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jun-12-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and moving parties John Rutgers and Mary Rutgers.
Donald Conway of VASQUEZ ESTRADA & CONWAY LLP (415-453-0555), appearing through CourtCall for defendant and non-opposing party SPX Cooling Technologies, Inc.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The Court did not consider the conditional non-opposition filed by Defendant SPX Cooling Technologies, Inc., F/K/A Marley Cooling Technologies, Inc. for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) The trial date is September 30, 2019 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 10, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 26, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 13, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 27, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 12, 2019. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Jun-12-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than June 26, 2019, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later then June 26, 2019, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on August 14, 2019 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than August 16, 2019. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 30, 2019. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by September 20, 2019 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, September 26, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
